Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The current model is Sequence create req approve req parallel block close case and the parallel block contains create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This allows all these activities to happen in any order including concurrently

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a proper Procure to Pay process there are dependencies Create Purchase Order should happen after Approve Purchase Requisition Receive Goods should happen after Create Purchase Order Receive Invoice should happen after Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO should happen after Receive Invoice Pay Invoice should happen after Match Invoice to PO

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The problem statement says simplified Procure to Pay process and lists steps in that order so the intended order is sequential

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the current models parallel block is wrong because it allows for example pay invoice before receive goods or receive invoice which is not allowed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus we need to enforce a sequence after PO we should have receive goods then receive invoice then match invoice then pay invoice then close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The corrected root sequence would be create req approve req create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: There is no parallelism needed all are required

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The fixed model uses a sequence operator to enforce the correct order ensuring that each step is completed before the next begins thus preventing unwanted behavior like paying before receiving goods or invoice
